title: Staggered spin susceptibility at a two-dimensional antiferromagnetic quantum critical point
abstract: We report on the finite temperature staggered spin susceptibility $χ(Q)$ as a function of the mode-mode coupling constant $y_1$ in the self-consistent renormalization theory of two-dimensional antiferromagnetic spin fluctuations with zero-point quantum fluctuations just at the quantum critical point ($y_0$ = 0). We find that the value $y_1$ = 0.1 is a criterion to classify the effect of the zero-point spin fluctuations on the temperature dependence of $χ(Q)$ into a Curie law for weak $y_1 < $ 0.1 and a Curie-Weiss type or a power law type for strong $y_1 > $ 0.1.
summary: This paper identifies a specific threshold in the mode-mode coupling constant that determines whether spin susceptibility follows a Curie law or a Curie-Weiss law at a 2D quantum critical point. It provides a new analytical expression for the Curie constant in the weak coupling limit. The work clarifies how the competition between zero-point and thermal fluctuations dictates the temperature dependence of magnetic properties.
main_problem: Determining how zero-point quantum fluctuations affect the temperature dependence of staggered spin susceptibility at a 2D antiferromagnetic quantum critical point.
main_result: Identified y1 = 0.1 as a critical threshold where the susceptibility transitions from a Curie law (weak coupling) to a Curie-Weiss or power-law type (strong coupling).
method: Numerical solution of the Watanabe-Miyake self-consistent renormalization (SCR) equation combined with least-squares fitting of power-law and linear functions.
model_or_system: A two-dimensional itinerant electron antiferromagnet at a quantum critical point (y0 = 0) modeled using the Watanabe-Miyake self-consistent renormalization theory.
key_observables: Staggered spin susceptibility chi(Q) and its inverse y.
important_parameters: Mode-mode coupling constant y1, reduced temperature t, and the cut-off wave number xc.
main_assumptions: The analysis is based on the self-consistent renormalization (SCR) theory including zero-point quantum fluctuations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 plots the reduced inverse susceptibility y against the reduced temperature t.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of quantum fluctuations at a QCP, describes the SCR model and numerical methods, presents the results for different coupling regimes, and concludes with a new analytical expression for the Curie constant.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2. (a) Log-log plots of y vs t. The broken lines are the least-squares ﬁtting results using the power law functions of y = AtB with ﬁt parameters of A and B. (b) Semi-logarithmic plots of the ﬁtting results of the numerical coeﬃcient A and the power B against y1. For y1 < 0.1, the value of B is approximately 1. As y1 exceeds 0.1, the power B increases beyond 1.

FIG. 1. Reduced inverse staggered spin susceptibility y plot- ted against reduced temperature t = T/T0 for the mode-mode coupling constant y1 = 0.005–10.0. The slope of y against t increases with increasing y1.
FIG. 4. Zero-point quantum ﬂuctuations Z(t), thermal ﬂuc- tuations H(t), and the summation Σ = Z(t) + H(t) plotted against t for y1 = 0.05 (< 0.1) and 1.0 (> 0.1).
